taxonomy: Ornus americana is a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Rosaceae, which consists of roses and other related plants. The genus Ornus is part of the subfamily Maloideae, which includes other fruit-bearing trees and shrubs. Ornus americana is also known as the American hawthorn, a common name shared with other species in the same genus. The classification of Ornus americana is subject to ongoing research and possible changes in botanical nomenclature.
uses: Ornus americana, also known as the American hawthorn, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The fruit and leaves of the plant are rich in antioxidants and have been studied for their potential health benefits. Ornus americana may help lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease due to its high content of flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins.
